title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure poses a significant threat to organizations relying on cloud services. By exploiting compromised CI/CD pipelines, attackers can deploy malicious code, potentially leading to data encryption, service disruption, and financial losses.
recommended_actions: Implement strict access controls and monitor CI/CD pipelines for unauthorized changes or suspicious activity.
Regularly back up critical data and ensure backups are stored in isolated environments to prevent ransomware propagation.
Deploy advanced threat detection tools to identify and mitigate ransomware activity targeting cloud environments.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself poses minimal direct risk; however, widespread adoption without proper vetting could lead to potential misuse, false positives, or reliance on incomplete vulnerability databases. Attackers may also analyze the tool to identify gaps or weaknesses.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the tool in a controlled environment to ensure its accuracy and reliability before integrating it into production workflows.
Cross-verify the tool's findings with other trusted vulnerability scanners to avoid false positives or missed vulnerabilities.
Monitor the tool's repository and community discussions for updates, patches, or potential security concerns.

title: A vulnerability was found in AstrBotDevs AstrBot up to 4.22.1. This issue affects the function...
description: A vulnerability was found in AstrBotDevs AstrBot up to 4.22.1. This issue affects the function install_plugin_upload of the file astrbot/dashboard/routes/plugin.py of the component install-upload Endpoint. The manipulation of the argument File results in sandbox issue. The attack can be executed remotely. The exploit has been made public and could be used. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-6117.

title: A vulnerability has been found in Totolink A7100RU 7.4cu.2313_b20191024. This vulnerability...
description: A vulnerability has been found in Totolink A7100RU 7.4cu.2313_b20191024. This vulnerability affects the function setDiagnosisCfg of the file /cgi-bin/cstecgi.cgi of the component CGI Handler. The manipulation of the argument ip leads to os command injection. Remote exploitation of the attack is possible. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Totolink A7100RU allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary OS commands via the CGI handler, potentially leading to full system compromise, unauthorized access, or further exploitation within the network.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply firmware updates or patches provided by Totolink to mitigate the vulnerability.
Restrict access to the affected device by implementing network segmentation and firewall rules to limit exposure.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity and implement intrusion detection systems to identify potential exploitation attempts.

Affected by this vulnerability is the function setTtyServiceCfg of the file /cgi-bin/cstecgi.cgi of the component CGI Handler. Such manipulation of the argument ttyEnable leads to os command injection. The attack can be launched remotely. The exploit has been disclosed publicly and may be used.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Totolink A7100RU router allows for remote OS command injection via the CGI handler. This could enable attackers to execute arbitrary commands, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data exfiltration, network compromise, or the deployment of malware.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply firmware updates or patches provided by Totolink to mitigate the vulnerability.
Disable or restrict access to the affected CGI handler endpoint (/cgi-bin/cstecgi.cgi) if a patch is not available.
Implement network segmentation and firewall rules to limit external access to the router's administrative interfaces.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

bpf: Fix unsound scalar...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

bpf: Fix unsound scalar forking in maybe_fork_scalars() for BPF_OR

maybe_fork_scalars() is called for both BPF_AND and BPF_OR when the
source operand is a constant.  When dst has signed range [-1, 0], it
forks the verifier state: the pushed path gets dst = 0, the current
path gets dst = -1.

For BPF_AND this is correct: 0 & K == 0.
For BPF_OR this is wrong:    0 | K == K, not 0.

The pushed path therefore tracks dst as 0 when

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability in the Linux kernel's BPF subsystem could lead to incorrect verifier state tracking, potentially allowing attackers to exploit the system for privilege escalation or bypass security mechanisms.
recommended_actions: Update Linux kernel to the latest patched version that resolves CVE-2026-31413.
Monitor and restrict the use of BPF programs in production environments until the patch is applied.
Perform a thorough audit of systems running BPF programs to identify potential exploitation attempts.

description: A weakness has been identified in Totolink A7100RU 7.4cu.2313_b20191024. Affected is the function setRadvdCfg of the file /cgi-bin/cstecgi.cgi of the component CGI Handler. This manipulation of the argument maxRtrAdvInterval causes os command injection. The attack can be initiated remotely. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary OS commands on the affected device, potentially leading to full system compromise, unauthorized access, and disruption of network services.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the firmware of Totolink A7100RU devices to the latest version if a patch is available.
Restrict access to the device's management interface by implementing network segmentation and firewall rules.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity and implement intrusion detection/prevention systems to identify potential exploitation attempts.

title: A Cross-site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability was identified in the `from_dict` method of the ...
description: A Cross-site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability was identified in the `from_dict` method of the `AppLollmsMessage` class in parisneo/lollms prior to version 2.2.0. The vulnerability arises from the lack of sanitization or HTML encoding of the `content` field when deserializing user-provided data. This allows an attacker to inject malicious HTML or JavaScript payloads, which can be executed in the context of another user's browser. Exploitation of this vulnerability can lead to account takeover, sessi

title: A vulnerability was determined in FoundationAgents MetaGPT up to 0.8.1. The impacted element is...
description: A vulnerability was determined in FoundationAgents MetaGPT up to 0.8.1. The impacted element is the function evaluateCode of the file metagpt/environment/minecraft/mineflayer/index.js of the component Mineflayer HTTP API. Executing a manipulation can lead to cross-site request forgery. The attack may be performed from remote. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may be utilized. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the MetaGPT Mineflayer HTTP API allows for cross-site request forgery (CSRF) attacks, which could enable attackers to execute unauthorized actions on behalf of authenticated users. This could lead to data manipulation, unauthorized access, or other malicious activities, especially in environments where the API is exposed to the internet.
recommended_actions: Immediately restrict public access to the vulnerable API endpoint by implementing network-level controls such as firewalls or IP whitelisting.
Apply CSRF protection mechanisms, such as requiring CSRF tokens for API requests, to mitigate the vulnerability.
Monitor for updates or patches from the FoundationAgents MetaGPT project and apply them as soon as they are available.

title: A reflected cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists in Rukovoditel CRM version 3.6.4 and...
description:
A reflected cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists in Rukovoditel CRM version 3.6.4 and earlier in the Zadarma telephony API endpoint (/api/tel/zadarma.php). The application directly reflects user-supplied input from the 'zd_echo' GET parameter into the HTTP response without proper sanitization, output encoding, or content-type restrictions.

The vulnerable code is:

if (isset($_GET['zd_echo'])) exit($_GET['zd_echo']);

An unauthenticated attacker can exploit this issue by crafting a ma

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The reflected XSS vulnerability in Rukovoditel CRM allows unauthenticated attackers to inject malicious scripts into the application's HTTP response. This can lead to session hijacking, phishing attacks, or unauthorized access to sensitive data, potentially compromising the integrity and confidentiality of the CRM system.
recommended_actions: Immediately update Rukovoditel CRM to the latest version if a patch is available, or apply vendor-recommended mitigations.
Implement input validation and output encoding for all user-supplied data to prevent XSS vulnerabilities.
Conduct a thorough security assessment of the application, focusing on other endpoints that may be vulnerable to similar issues.

title: Various stored XSS vulnerabilities in the maps- and icon rendering logic in Phoca Maps component...
description: Various stored XSS vulnerabilities in the maps- and icon rendering logic in Phoca Maps component 5.0.0-6.0.2 have been discovered.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The stored XSS vulnerabilities in the Phoca Maps component could allow attackers to inject malicious scripts into the application, potentially leading to data theft, session hijacking, or unauthorized access to sensitive information. This could impact both users and administrators of the affected system.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the Phoca Maps component to the latest patched version, if available, or apply any recommended security patches from the vendor.
Implement input validation and output sanitization to prevent malicious scripts from being executed.
Conduct a thorough security audit of the application to identify and mitigate other potential vulnerabilities.

title: CVE-2026-5590 - A race condition during TCP connection teardown can cause tcp_recv() to operate on a connection that
description: A race condition during TCP connection teardown can cause tcp_recv() to operate on a connection that has already been released. If tcp_conn_search() returns NULL while processing a SYN packet, a NULL pointer derived from stale context data is passed to tcp_backlog_is_full() and dereferenced without validation, leading to a crash.
